1_tone_matters.insight: Same structure can be playful OR malicious depending on tone, acting, dialogue
examples: take-your-time: NOT malicious compliance, playful/absurdist with soft inviting tone
hurry-chant: Pettiness shown through strange clapping, mean-spirited undertone
1_tone_matters.training_implication: Model must read TONE not just STRUCTURE

2_scenographic_techniques.insight: POV shots + sound perspective changes imply internal dialogue without explicit narration
examples: tip-pov-flip: Camera POV + sound perspective shift = viewer assumes internal thoughts
2_scenographic_techniques.training_implication: Teach model to recognize cinematic conventions that imply interiority

3_social_invitation_violation.insight: Humor from unexpected violation of implicit social contracts/invitations
examples: rigged-bottle-flip: Fun game invitation → surprising physical sabotage + meanness
tack-detsamma: Polite ritual → absurdist literal acceptance (like someone who doesn't understand boundaries)
3_social_invitation_violation.training_implication: Social scripts have implicit 'invitations' that can be violated

4_viewer_emotional_engagement.insight: Some humor works by creating viewer sympathy/disappointment, not just surprise
examples: regular-walks-past: Viewer feels bad for server's wasted hopeful gesture
extreme-gratitude: Camera = viewer POV, kiss feels literally directed at viewer
4_viewer_emotional_engagement.training_implication: Not all humor is intellectual - some is empathetic

5_world_building_absurdism.insight: Some clips create 'dream-like' worlds where normal rules don't apply
examples: colleague-gets-extra: Nonchalant rule-breaking frames business as 'opportunistic wonderland'
elderly-makes-own: Acting + tempo + escalation creates engaging absurdist world
5_world_building_absurdism.training_implication: Absurdism isn't just 'weird' - it's coherent world-building with different rules

6_inference_requirements.insight: Some patterns require multi-step inference, not just recognition
examples: flip-container: Must infer that flipping reveals hidden chicken
gen-z-quit-trigger: Requires cultural inference about generational stereotypes
6_inference_requirements.training_implication: Model needs to show REASONING PATH, not just conclusion

7_character_framing.insight: Same action can frame character as clever OR stupid depending on context
examples: pays-with-her-card: Frames man as stupid, not just 'inversion'
waitress-hammer: Absurdist reaction to frustration, not threat
7_character_framing.training_implication: Character interpretation depends on surrounding context

8_acting_and_execution.insight: Physical comedy quality depends heavily on acting, tempo, slapstick execution
examples: elderly-makes-own: Funny because of acting quality and escalating wildness
pizza-not-finished: Chef's 'funny small scream like a bird' is key to tone
8_acting_and_execution.training_implication: Some humor is performance-dependent, not just structure-dependent

waitress-hammer.corrected: Sketch about service rejection → complaint → absurdist frustration reaction (hammer as hypothetical confrontation symbol)

take-your-time-malicious.corrected: Playful/absurdist reaction to polite phrase, NOT malicious

creature-hunt-beer.corrected: Responsible action toward 'found' animal (like bird in kitchen), beer reveal adds silliness

new_pattern_suggestions.tone_dependent_interpretation: Same structure reads differently based on acting/dialogue tone
new_pattern_suggestions.cinematic_interiority: POV + sound perspective implies internal thoughts
new_pattern_suggestions.social_invitation_violation: Breaking implicit contracts within social rituals
new_pattern_suggestions.empathetic_humor: Humor through viewer sympathy, not just surprise
new_pattern_suggestions.coherent_absurdist_world: World-building where different rules apply consistently
